file_name: NETGEAR,INC_04_21_2003-EX-10.16- AMENDMENT #2 TO THE DISTRIBUTION AGREEMENT.txt

perturbation.original_text: This Amendment is entered into effective October 1,1996 ("Amendment Date") by and between NETGEAR, Inc. ("NETGEAR"), a wholly owned subsidiary of Bay Networks, Inc., and Ingram Micro ("Distributor") acting on behalf of itself and its affiliates. NETGEAR and Distributor having previously entered into a Distributor Agreement ("Agreement") with an Effective Date of March 1,1996, now mutually agree to amend that Agreement as follows:
perturbation.changed_text: This Amendment is entered into effective October 1,1996 ("Amendment Date") by and between NETGEAR, Inc. ("NETGEAR"), a wholly owned subsidiary of Bay Networks, Inc., and Ingram Micro ("Retailer") acting on behalf of itself and its affiliates. NETGEAR and Retailer having previously entered into a Distributor Agreement ("Agreement") with an Effective Date of March 1,1996, now mutually agree to amend that Agreement as follows:
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Distributor' to 'Retailer' creates confusion because the original agreement refers to Ingram Micro as a 'Distributor.' This creates an internal contradiction. The agreement then continues using 'Distributor' without re-defining the term. This implies Retailer and Distributor are meant as the same, but nowhere specifies this is indeed the case.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Amendment is entered into effective October 1,1996 ("Amendment Date") by and between NETGEAR, Inc. ("NETGEAR"), a wholly owned subsidiary of Bay Networks, Inc., and Ingram Micro ("Retailer") acting on behalf of itself and its affiliates. NETGEAR and Retailer having previously entered into a Distributor Agreement ("Agreement") with an Effective Date of March 1,1996, now mutually agree to amend that Agreement as follows:
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Distributor' with 'Retailer' while the rest of the agreement still refers to 'Distributor' based on the prior agreement. This introduces a misaligned terminology issue where the contract uses both 'Distributor' and 'Retailer' but does not explicitly define them as interchangeable, thus causing confusion regarding the obligations and rights. This falls under the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'. Changing the role from Distributor to Retailer directly affects the duties and rights the involved party has, thus it creates a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: For the purposes of this provision, distributor shall mean a company acquiring products directly from NETGEAR for resale or license to dealers or other second tier resellers which in turn resell or license the products to end use customers.
perturbation.changed_text: For the purposes of this provision, seller shall mean a company acquiring products directly from NETGEAR for resale or license to dealers or other second tier resellers which in turn resell or license the products to end use customers.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'distributor' to 'seller' creates confusion on how the agreement is defined and creates an internal contradiction. The original agreement refers to NETGEAR as a 'Distributor.' This creates an in-text contradiction due to the changed reference and the fact that the original terms of distributor are still used throught the text
perturbation.contradicted_text: During the initial one year period beginning on the Amendment Date, Distributor shall be the only distributor appointed by NETGEAR in [*], subject to Distributor conducting mutually agreed to marketing activities as described in the Marketing Plan for [*] to be developed and agreed to by and between the parties and which shall be attached to and made a part of this Agreement as Exhibit 4a. For the purposes of this provision, seller shall mean a company acquiring products directly from NETGEAR for resale or license to dealers or other second tier resellers which in turn resell or license the products to end use customers. The foregoing notwithstanding, during the [*] and any subsequent period, NETGEAR reserves the right to sell or license Products in [*] to customers other than distributors such as, but not limited to resellers who procure Products at centralized locations for resale to end-use customers solely through their wholly or majority owned retail outlets, both store-front and catalog. Following the [*], for any extension or renewal term, Distributor's appointment as Distributor shall be non-exclusive and NETGEAR may appoint other distributors in [*] at its sole discretion.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'distributor' to 'seller' directly contradicts the contradicted text. The contradicted text explicitly identifies 'Distributor' as the entity subject to certain conditions and obligations ('During the initial one year period beginning on the Amendment Date, Distributor shall be the only distributor appointed by NETGEAR'). By redefining 'distributor' as 'seller' within the same provision, the modification creates ambiguity and contradiction regarding the application of the obligations and exclusivity previously assigned to the 'Distributor'. This falls under 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' as it uses 'seller' and 'distributor' interchangeably and without proper definition, leading to conflicting obligations, because it is unclear if the exclusivity applies to 'seller' now as well.

perturbation.original_text: The undersigned has read this Amendment, agrees hereto, and is an authorized representative of its respective party. INGRAM MICRO INC. NETGEAR, INC. 1600 East St. Andrew Place 4401 Great America Parkway Santa Ana, CA 92705 P.O. Box 58185 Santa Clara, CA 95052-8185 By: /S/ AC Mann By: /s/ PATRICK Lo ---------------- -------------------- Name: AC Mann Name: PATRICK Lo Title: VP Purchasing Title: V. P. Netgear, Inc.
perturbation.changed_text: The undersigned has read this Amendment, agrees hereto, and is an authorized representative of its respective party. INGRAM MICRO INC. NETGEAR, INC. 1600 East St. Andrew Place 4401 Great America Parkway Santa Ana, CA 92705 P.O. Box 58185 Santa Clara, CA 95052-8185 By: /S/ AC Mann By: /s/ PATRICK Lo ---------------- -------------------- Name: AC Mann Name: PATRICK Lo Title: Purchasing Manager Title: V. P. Netgear, Inc.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'VP Purchasing' to 'Purchasing Manager' creates an uncertainty about the signatory's authority and whether the Purchasing Manager has the authority to bind Ingram Micro, contradicting the assertion that they are 'an authorized representative of its respective party'. This affects the validity of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The undersigned has read this Amendment, agrees hereto, and is an authorized representative of its respective party. INGRAM MICRO INC. NETGEAR, INC. 1600 East St. Andrew Place 4401 Great America Parkway Santa Ana, CA 92705 P.O. Box 58185 Santa Clara, CA 95052-8185 By: /S/ AC Mann By: /s/ PATRICK Lo ---------------- -------------------- Name: AC Mann Name: PATRICK Lo Title: Purchasing Manager Title: V. P. Netgear, Inc.
perturbation.justification: The modified text changes 'VP Purchasing' to 'Purchasing Manager' for AC Mann. This alteration creates a direct contradiction because it casts doubt on whether the signatory, now identified as 'Purchasing Manager,' possesses the necessary authority to bind Ingram Micro. The contradicted text states that the undersigned is an 'authorized representative,' and the change in title undermines this assertion, leading to a strong in-text contradiction regarding the validity and enforceability of the agreement. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.
